mnemonics: DECODER: Determines Exactly One Code Outputs Result
Think of a decoder like a hotel concierge: you give them a room number (input), and they point you to the exact one correct door (output line).
keyPoints: A decoder activates exactly one output line for a given binary input code.
An n-to-2^n decoder has n inputs and 2^n outputs.
Decoders can be combined and used as fundamental building blocks for constructing larger logic systems, like memory address selection.
commonMistakes: Assuming multiple outputs can be active (high) at the same time for a single input code; only one output is active at a time.
Forgetting that the unused (or disabled) outputs are in their inactive state (usually logic 0), not a high-impedance 'floating' state.
